rulings.Case Title: PHUONG T. NGUYEN VS. LONDON BREED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 7, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is continued to September 15, 2023. No later than September 1, 2023 the moving party to submit a supplemental brief not to exceed 3 pages providing authority for the asserted "special appearances" of the defendants who have previously generally appeared (PUC filed a demurrer to the original complaint) and who have obtained substantive relief on the merits as to the sufficiency of Plaintiff's complaint on their demurrer to the First Amended Complaint and are seeking substantive relief on the merits as to the Second Amended Complaint (PUC, Teague, Choi, Tam and Borovina). Moving party is ordered AGAIN to submit a proposed order on the Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint heard on September 20, 2021. Moving party shall comply with the instructions contained in April 18, 2023 rejection letter and submit a declaration of compliance along with a proposed order no later than September 1, 2023. Moving party shall also submit a proposed order on the Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint heard on April 14, 2023 in compliance with instructions contained in April 18, 2023 rejection letter. A declaration of compliance and proposed order must be submitted to the Court no later than September 1, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: SAAM BARRAGER ET AL VS. LAURA DOUGLASS DECARAVA
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 7, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT LAURA DECARAVA MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendant moves to strike the entire complaint, or alternatively specified paragraphs of the complaint per CCP 425.16. Motion to strike the entire complaint is DENIED. Moving party fails to address how the entire complaint and each claim contained therein arises out of protected activity. Motion to Strike specified paragraphs of the complaint upon which each of the asserted claims in the complaint is based is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Each of plaintiff's claims arise out of allegations contained in paragraphs 4-38 as is expressly alleged in the Complaint (see paragraphs 41, 46, 51, 56, 61, 66). Defendant argues that any claim arising out of the rent board petition, police reports and application for a civil harassment is subject to CCP 425.16. Defendant shifted its burden on the first prong of the statute. However, Plaintiff has shown probability of prevailing on the merits as to some of the claims. The motion to strike is denied (1) as it pertains to the claim for abuse of process re: violation of a restraining order; See Siam v. Kizilbash (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 1563; (2) the motion is denied as to all claims arising of false police reports CC 47(b)(5). Plaintiff's Reliance on Hagberg v. Cal. Fed. Bank, decided 17 years before he enactment of CC 47(b)(5) is not supported by any authority. Hagberg v. California Federal Bank (2004) 32 Cal.4th 350. Motion to strike is granted (1) as to the claim for malicious prosecution as such claim may not be based on a civil harassment action as a matter of law. Siam v. Kizilbash (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 1563; and as to (2) the following paragraphs of the complaint: Paragraph 11 in its entirety; 20 in its entirety; 24 the first and the third sentences only; 28 the second and the third sentence only; 31 in its entirety, 37 in its entirety; 38 the words :denying Defendant's restraining order, and" only. Attorney's fees granted in the amount of $4,400. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SAAM BARRAGER ET AL VS. LAURA DOUGLASS DECARAVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 7, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT LAURA DECARAVA Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Judgment on the Pleadings as to the claim for fraud is granted with leave to amend for Plaintiff to assert this claim as a separately labeled cause of action and to allege facts in support of each element of this cause of action. Judgment on the Pleading based on issue preclusion is DENIED. The Court takes judicial notice of the filings, including the judgment in the unlawful detainer case, CUD-22-668828. Moving party failed to show that in the unlawful detainer action the issues presented "were actually litigated and necessary for a determination of the lawsuit." Unlawful detainer actions include procedural and substantive components, all of which were raised in the action and addressed in the Jury Instructions. (e.g. 4305; 4308(4); 4309; 4324; 4325(2)). Nothing on the face of the Judgment indicates what the basis for the jury verdict was. The judgment merely states that Defendant is the prevailing party in an unlawful detainer action. The sole issue on a claim for unlawful detainer is possession.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
